beats.title: THE ARMORY
beats.subtitle: Control Baselines & Tailoring · NIST SP 800-53B
say: You know the system's worth. Now draw your loadout from the armory.
The baselines, and the tailoring guidance, live in SP 800-53B.

beats.title: What is a baseline?
bullets: A pre-defined STARTING set of controls for an impact level
Three security baselines: Low, Moderate, High
Plus a separate Privacy baseline for systems that process PII
Your FIPS 199 level picks the SECURITY baseline: a Moderate system → Moderate baseline
say: So a baseline is a ready-made kit?
A starting kit. Matched to how much the system matters.

beats.plain: Tailoring is adjusting the baseline to fit your system — removing what doesn't apply, adding what's missing, setting parameters, and justifying each change. An overlay is ready-made tailoring for a whole community.
say: Tailoring fits the kit to you — like Aegis Hospital dropping wireless because that app is wired-only, and adding a healthcare overlay. An overlay is shared tailoring, so you don't start from scratch.

beats.title: The three security baselines (controls + enhancements)
say: Higher impact, more controls and enhancements, by design.

beats.title: Then you TAILOR it
bullets: Scoping — drop controls that don't apply to your system
Compensating controls — swap in an alternative that meets the intent
Parameters — fill in organization-defined values (frequencies, roles)
Supplementation — ADD controls for risks the baseline misses
say: A baseline is a starting point, never the finish line. You tailor it to fit.
I look for the control you wrongly tailored away.

beats.title: Overlays & a key nuance
bullets: Overlays — ready-made tailored baselines for a community (cloud, classified, mission-specific)
The Privacy baseline (96 controls) covers systems processing PII
Security baselines span 18 families — Program Management (PM) is org-wide, not in them
say: Overlays are shared tailoring, so you don't start from scratch.
And remember, PM controls run across the whole organization, outside any baseline.

beats.q: Where do the Low / Moderate / High baselines live?
say: Quick check. Which book holds the baselines? Pause, and answer.

beats.q: Adjusting a baseline — scoping out, swapping, adding controls — is called?
say: And the act of adjusting the baseline to fit your system?

beats.title: Episode 10 — remember this
bullets: Your FIPS 199 category selects the baseline
Then TAILOR: scope, compensate, set parameters, supplement, apply overlays
say: Defenses chosen and fitted. Now, do they actually work?
Next: the reckoning, assessment and authorization.
